questions.prompt: Which of the following describes the fastest storage locations available to the CPU for executing instructions?

choices.label: Main Memory (RAM)
choices.reasoning: Main memory is much slower than the CPU registers and caches, so it is not the fastest storage location.

choices.label: Registers
choices.reasoning: Registers are located directly within the CPU and provide the lowest latency for data access.

choices.label: Virtual Memory
choices.reasoning: Virtual memory is an abstraction using disk and RAM, which is far slower than CPU register storage.

choices.label: Level 1 Cache
choices.reasoning: L1 cache is very fast, but CPU registers are still faster and are the closest storage to execution units.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are common characteristics of RISC CPU architectures?

choices.label: Simple instruction set with fixed-length instructions
choices.reasoning: RISC architectures favor a small set of simple, fixed-length instructions for faster decoding and pipelining.

choices.label: Complex addressing modes and microcoded operations
choices.reasoning: Complex addressing modes are more commonly associated with CISC architectures.

choices.label: A large number of general-purpose registers
choices.reasoning: RISC designs typically provide many registers to reduce memory access and improve performance.

choices.label: Default support for variable-length instruction formats
choices.reasoning: Variable-length instruction formats are more common in CISC designs, not RISC.

questions.prompt: Select all x86 general-purpose registers that can hold integer values.

choices.label: EAX
choices.reasoning: EAX is a general-purpose integer register in x86 architectures.

choices.label: EBX
choices.reasoning: EBX is also a general-purpose integer register on x86.

choices.label: ST0
choices.reasoning: ST0 is a floating-point stack register, not a general-purpose integer register.

choices.label: ESI
choices.reasoning: ESI is a general-purpose register used for string and memory operations.

questions.prompt: Write a short pseudocode algorithm to swap the values of variables a and b using a temporary variable.
